name: MuleSoft Anypoint Platform API
what_it_does: MuleSoft Anypoint Platform REST API for enterprise integration and API management platform (Salesforce subsidiary). Enables AI agents to manage API design and publishing in Anypoint Exchange, handle application deployment and monitoring on CloudHub and Runtime Fabric, access API Analytics and usage metrics, retrieve connector and integration asset management data, manage API policies and security gateway configuration, handle Runtime Manager and server management, access DataGraph and unified data layer queries, retrieve Flex Gateway and edge API management data, manage access management and user permissions, and integrate with Salesforce Platform and enterprise systems.
use_cases: Managing API deployments and runtime monitoring from platform engineering agents
Accessing integration analytics and usage data from API governance agents
Handling connector and asset management from integration development agents
Integrating MuleSoft with Salesforce and enterprise systems from iPaaS automation agents
not_for: Simple point-to-point integrations without enterprise iPaaS requirements
Small teams without Salesforce/MuleSoft enterprise licensing budget
API publishing without enterprise API lifecycle management
best_when: An enterprise using MuleSoft Anypoint Platform for API management and integration wants AI agents to automate deployment pipelines, API governance, analytics monitoring, and integration with Salesforce and enterprise systems.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: MuleSoft manages enterprise integration flows for production systems — automated deployment and configuration changes can disrupt live integrations. API policy changes affect all consuming applications. CloudHub/Runtime Fabric configuration requires infrastructure expertise. Enterprise data flows through MuleSoft may include PII and financial data.

auth.notes: MuleSoft Anypoint uses OAuth 2.0 for platform API access. Connected app credentials with organization, environment, and asset scopes. Anypoint Platform documentation with API reference. Webhooks for deployment and alert events. Salesforce-owned since 2018 with Salesforce platform integration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise Anypoint Platform subscription required
pricing.paid_tiers: Core, Gold, Platinum, Titanium enterprise subscriptions; vCore-based CloudHub pricing
pricing.notes: San Francisco, California. Subsidiary of Salesforce (acquired 2018 for $6.5B). Founded 2006. Market leader for enterprise iPaaS and API management. $1B+ annual revenue within Salesforce. 2,000+ enterprise customers. Anypoint Exchange has 500+ connectors. Competes with IBM MQ, TIBCO, and Boomi for enterprise integration.

performance.rate_limits: Platform API: rate limits per connected app; runtime varies by deployment

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Automated deployment and policy changes affect live enterprise integrations — require staging and approval
CloudHub vCore pricing — automated deployment agent must monitor resource consumption to avoid cost overruns
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API with Anypoint Platform documentation
DataGraph requires GraphQL knowledge — separate API surface from REST management API
Salesforce-owned — Salesforce platform licensing required for deep Salesforce integration features
Integration data flows include PII and financial data — connected app permissions must be narrowly scoped
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise integration and API management. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP, HIPAA, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. Multi-region. Enterprise data flows and API governance sensitivity.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise integration platform. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP, HIPAA, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. Multi-region. Enterprise data flow and API governance.
